narration: Import the random module to enable random word selection.
Set the coding language to Python in Visual Studio Code to avoid plain text mode.
Create a list named animals containing words to guess from.
Populate the animals list with sample words like dolphin, shark, elephant, and rhino.
Define a variable word using random.choice(animals) and convert it to lowercase.
Initialize an empty list called guess_correctly to store correct guesses.
Initialize an empty list called guess_incorrectly to store incorrect guesses.
Create a variable tries set to 6 to limit the number of guesses.
Create a variable hangman_count initialized to 1 to track the hangman drawing progress.
Define a while loop that continues as long as tries is greater than zero.
Inside the loop, create an if statement to check if the guessed letter is in guess_correctly.
If the letter is correct, append it to the output string.
If the letter is incorrect, append an underscore to the output string.
Define an output variable initialized to an empty string.
Add an if statement to break the program if output equals the word.
Print instructions to the user to guess the word.
Print the current output string and the number of tries remaining.
Create a variable guess using input() and convert it to lowercase.
Check if the guessed letter is already in guess_correctly or guess_incorrectly.
If the letter is in the word, congratulate the player and add it to output.
If the letter is not in the word, inform the user they guessed a wrong letter.
Increment hangman_count by 1 when an incorrect guess is made.
Decrement tries by 1 after each guess attempt.
Append the guessed letter to guess_correctly or guess_incorrectly based on correctness.
Check if tries is still greater than zero after a guess.
If the word is guessed correctly, inform the user they won.
If the word is not guessed and tries equal one, inform the user to try again.
Print the current hangman_count to display the drawing progress.
Run the program to test the Hangman game logic.
Create a list of strings representing the hangman drawing stages.
Define the first stage of the hangman drawing with a base and crossbar.
Copy and paste the first stage to define the second stage.
Add the head to the second stage of the hangman drawing.
Add the upper body to the third stage of the hangman drawing.
Add the first arm to the third stage of the hangman drawing.
Add the second arm to the fourth stage of the hangman drawing.
Add the legs to the fifth stage of the hangman drawing.
Close the string quotes for the complete hangman drawing list.
Update the print function to display the hangman drawing based on hangman_count.
